title: Restriction preserves monotonicity
nl_statement: If f is monotone, then every restriction of f is monotone.

title: Restriction semantics agrees with restricted tree semantics
nl_statement: Evaluating a restricted tree matches evaluating the original tree on assignments extended by the restriction.
notes: Core semantic theorem used by the falsifier and later proofs.

title: Uniform error of identical trees is zero
nl_statement: A decision tree has zero uniform error against itself.

title: Uniform error is symmetric
nl_statement: Uniform disagreement is symmetric in its two arguments.
notes: Good next wrapper theorem after the active claim.

title: Influence of constant false is zero
nl_statement: The influence of any variable on the constant-false function is zero.

title: Influence of constant true is zero
nl_statement: The influence of any variable on the constant-true function is zero.

title: Unused variables have zero influence
nl_statement: If a variable does not occur in a tree, then its influence on the tree's Boolean function is zero.
notes: Good follow-on active theorem family after ST-06.

title: Restriction cannot introduce new variables
nl_statement: The variables used by a restricted tree are contained in the unrestricted variables minus fixed coordinates.
notes: Executable set-inclusion lemma for bounded search.

title: Leaves compute monotone functions
nl_statement: Constant-leaf trees compute monotone Boolean functions.

title: Greedy split conjecture shell
nl_statement: A suitable split heuristic decreases error on some monotone target family.
notes: Reserved for later claim intake once the formal substrate is larger.
